content.claim: Hungary exhibits the highest measured school segregation in the EU/OECD: the 2022 PISA study found Hungary has the highest segregation of disadvantaged students into different schools compared to other EU countries, and segregation in Hungary occurs primarily through intentional separation of higher-status pupils rather than active ethnic separation, with academic literature explicitly framing early-selection tracks as segregation mechanisms.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Causal attribution is contested: it is unresolved whether the 6-/8-year structure causes segregation or channels pre-existing sorting demand that would occur under alternative track structures
The literature documents the mechanism (elite-track selection) but does not quantify its proportional contribution to total segregation outcomes
Cross-country comparison may reflect differences in data collection methodology
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
